current.summary: Integrated trust anchor design into ACP spec v0.2.0, ran 5-agent panel review (2 rounds), identified 13 consensus fixes.

activeWork.description: ACP spec v0.2.0 trust anchor integration complete. Panel review identified 13 fixes (8 consensus, 5 additional). All fixes documented and ready for implementation.
activeWork.progress: 1) Read ecosystem-landscape.md for context. 2) Rewrote Section 2 (platform-first framing, trust chain narrative, overview). 3) Added 3 new fields: publisher_identity (6.3.15), repository_owner_id (6.3.16), sigstore_trust_root (6.3.17). 4) Updated Section 8.1 hashed fields allowlist + Section 8.2 type mappings. 5) Added Section 9.2 steps 6 (source binding) and 7 (publisher_identity check) + TOFU note. 6) Added Sections 11.17-11.19 (residual risks, rollback, TOFU). 7) Fixed Appendix C Forgejo entry. 8) Added Appendix D (OIDC extensions, enterprise Sigstore, sigstore-a2a). 9) Added TV-MH4 test vector (hash verified). 10) Updated CHANGELOG.md. 11) Ran 5-agent adversarial panel review (2 rounds, 48 findings, 13 consensus fixes identified). 12) Updated acp-trust-anchor.md intro.

git.lastCommit: aa1fa3f docs: TUI confirm-bucket rendering design — 6 panel reviews

recentDecisions: Bumped spec to v0.2.0 (not meta_version — schema stays at 1, new fields are optional).
sigstore_trust_root excluded from meta_hash (distribution metadata, not content provenance — allows same meta.yaml to be served by different registries).
Forgejo removed from supported platforms (cannot sign via OIDC — no id_tokens, no Fulcio issuer).
Section 2 restructured into 2.1 (who implements), 2.2 (trust chain), 2.3 (overview) — per ecosystem landscape positioning.
Panel review found 13 consensus fixes needed before publishing.
nextSteps: Implement all 13 panel fixes in acp-spec.md. The fixes are organized in two tiers: 8 consensus fixes (5-0 agreement) and 5 additional fixes (3+ votes). See detailed list below.
Fix #4 requires recomputing TV-MH4's hash (change source_repo from 4-segment to 3-segment path, or update 6.3.8 to allow extended paths).
After fixes: commit all ACP spec changes (acp-spec.md, CHANGELOG.md, acp-trust-anchor.md, panel artifacts).

context.notes:
PANEL REVIEW FIXES — implement these in order:

CONSENSUS (5-0, must fix):
1. Section 6.3.15 + 9.2 step 7: Add normative text that publisher_identity is self-reported, MUST NOT be treated as verified identity. Add 'Consumers MUST NOT treat absence of publisher_identity as proof of direct publishing.'
2. Section 9.2 + 6.3.17: Add SHOULD to 9.2 for sigstore_trust_root usage when present. Add security note that field is not integrity-protected and MUST NOT be blindly trusted.
3. Section 9.2 step 5: Demote MUST to SHOULD. Add 'Strict consumers MUST document their identity verification algorithm.'
4. TV-MH4 / 6.3.8: Fix source_repo to 3-segment path (simplest) or update 6.3.8 to permit extended paths. Recompute hash.
5. Section 6.3.15: Move 'differs' definition from informative Note to normative text body.
6. Section 6.4: Add sigstore_trust_root row to scope table.
7. Section 11.18: Downgrade 'Registries SHOULD maintain signed latest-version manifest' to non-normative recommendation.
8. Section 11.19 + 5.3: Demote TOFU MUST to registry-side SHOULD. Add first-publish policy requirement to 5.3.

ADDITIONAL (3+ votes):
9. Section 9.2 step 4: Add Fulcio certificate expiry guidance (verify against Rekor timestamp, not current time).
10. Section 9.2 step 7: Clarify 'surface to user' includes machine-readable audit output for headless CI/CD.
11. Section 9.2 step 6: Add normative behavior when OID 1.3.6.1.4.1.57264.1.12 is absent from certificate.
12. Section 5.2.2: Mirror strict consumer source binding requirement from 9.2.
13. Appendix D.3: Remove RFC 2119 SHOULD (applies to third-party spec), use plain prose.

Panel message bus: docs/spec/acp/panel/v0.2.0-review-bus.jsonl
Full panel findings are in the bus — each panelist's Round 1 has detailed rationale and suggested fix text for every finding.
